title: Jean-Georges Vongerichten restaurants in New York's Tin Building have shuttered
summary: The $200 million project on the Manhattan waterfront is to be converted into the Balloon Museum, ending an attempt to turn the Seaport into a dining destination.

title: Jack in the Box beats Biglari, but names a new chairman anyway
summary: The fast-food chain’s shareholders voted to re-elect all 10 directors, beating back a “vote no” effort on Chairman David Goebel. But it named a former Taco Bell CEO its new chairman.

title: The secret behind the success of Dave's Hot Chicken
summary: A Deeper Dive: Jim Bitticks, CEO of the fast-casual hot-chicken chain, joins the restaurant finance podcast to talk about his new role and why the brand has been so successful.

title: How 4 restaurant chains are using AI today
summary: AI is now part of the team at Krispy Kreme, Freddy’s, Taco John’s and Nekter, and the applications are as diverse as the brands themselves.

title: Cracker Barrel sees some progress in rebound from logo saga
summary: Traffic is still down significantly at the family-dining chain, but it is beginning to improve, and customers are giving the chain higher marks on food and service.

title: Starbucks plans to open a corporate office in Tennessee
summary: The coffee shop giant, which wants to expand in the Southeastern U.S., plans to open the facility later this year. It is the latest major restaurant chain to open a corporate office in the state.

title: Dutch Bros' first Los Angeles location has everything but the drive-thru
summary: The beverage chain’s “walk-thru” shop near the University of Southern California campus could demonstrate its ability to work in more urban markets. That could change the chain’s growth potential.

title: Cheese is melting all over menus, giving sales a solid boost
summary: It started with Chili’s and those viral cheese pulls. Now other casual-dining chains are cashing in on the allure of ooey, gooey cheese.

title: Keith Lee jumps from restaurant influencer to investor
summary: The famed social media reviewer has made his first restaurant investment in Brooklyn Dumpling Shop in a multi-year partnership.

title: Olo is launching a customer-facing ordering app
summary: The Olo App will feature hundreds of restaurant brands, giving customers a “remote control” for food ordering. Restaurants will pay no commissions and will get access to customer data from across the network.

title: March marketing tie-ins drive menus
summary: Taste Tracker: Starbucks, Chili’s, Firehouse Subs, Lazy Dog, Sonic, Buffalo Wild Wings, Domino’s, Whataburger, KFC and more headline restaurant menu news and trends.

title: JRI Hospitality buys 43 more Freddy's restaurants
summary: The Salina, Kansas-based operator is already the largest operator in the fast-casual burger chain’s system and will now operate more than 130 restaurants in 18 states.

title: Sweetgreen wanted to reach 1,000 units by the end of the decade. It may take a bit longer
summary: The fast-casual salad chain slowed its pace of growth significantly this year. Now the company is reviewing its portfolio. Is shrinkage in Sweetgreen's future?

title: Pilot Travel Centers launch robust new foodservice program
summary: In addition to expanding its prepared foods and grab-and-go offerings, the country's largest travel center operator will also keep its franchisee-run fast-food restaurants open 24/7.

title: Chef Sungchul Shim infuses Korean flavor into a Times Square steakhouse
summary: On the Menu: Gui Steakhouse has all the bells and whistles of a traditional restaurant in this genre, but it’s the Korean accents that set it apart.

title: The burger wars get some new life, thanks to social media
summary: McDonald’s CEO Chris Kempczinski was roasted online for taking a small bite of the chain’s new Big Arch Burger on video. Burger King, which just upgraded its Whopper, did not let it pass.

title: Winners and losers from a strange end to 2025
summary: The Bottom Line: Performance varied greatly among chains that have reported their end-of-year earnings thus far, with some big winners and big losers.

title: A smaller Steak n Shake finds its footing
summary: The burger chain has transformed its business model and started marketing to the MAGA crowd with a quality message. But it closed more stores, and the chain’s franchisees are apparently struggling.

title: An operator couldn't find the tech he needed, so he built it with AI
summary: Tech Check: Pincho CEO Otto Othman is now offering his "vibe-coded" recipe manager to other restaurants. He believes tech suppliers should be concerned about the rise of AI coding tools.

title: Life happens at Norms. So, apparently, do movies
summary: In the movie "Good Luck, Have Fun, Don't Die," unlikely heroes are found at the LA diner chain when the world needs saving. It's the latest movie role for the restaurant chain that is working on its own happily-ever-after story.

title: Patrick Doyle believes Restaurant Brands International is undervalued
summary: The Bottom Line: The executive chairman of the owner of Burger King, Tim Hortons, Popeyes and Firehouse Subs made the case to investors as the company gets back to franchising.

title: Slutty Vegan founder Pinky Cole files for bankruptcy
summary: The entrepreneur behind the plant-based burger brand owes nearly $1.4 million in debt and back taxes, according to court documents.

title: The problem with the pizza market
summary: The Bottom Line: This week’s edition of the weekly restaurant finance newsletter examines the weakness of the fast-food pizza market, as both Papa Johns and Pizza Hut have challenges.

title: It's time for banh mi to go national, says this Vietnamese sandwich pioneer
summary: Bun Mee was founded in San Francisco in 2011 and now has five Bay Area locations. A franchisee is bringing the fast-casual brand to New York City for the first time.

title: El Pollo Loco, Eggs Up Grill and more make leadership moves
summary: Executive Summary: The latest leadership updates in the restaurant industry.

title: Struggling Sweetgreen is rethinking its menu pricing
summary: The fast-casual salad chain's same-store sales plunged 11.5% in the fourth quarter and 7.9% for the year, prompting executives to consider simplifying its cost structure.

title: Burger King's store-level profitability runs into a beef cost wall
summary: The Bottom Line: The fast-food chain has been improving per-store profitability in recent years as it works to solidify its performance. But beef prices had something to say about that.

title: The beverage boom is transforming the soda fountain
summary: As restaurants look to meet growing demand for specialty beverages, they are tapping equipment suppliers for new products. The result is a new generation of high-tech drink machines.

title: How Shake Shack improved profits despite soaring beef costs
summary: The fast-casual chain grew margins, despite a challenging macroeconomic climate in 2025. CEO Rob Lynch said the ongoing work on operations paid off and set the stage for a stronger 2026.

title: At this Southern California car dealership, you can get a patty melt with your Porsche
summary: A car dealer is building a $5 million restaurant empire in its showrooms. Guests love the quirky aspect of finding a "hidden gem" where they least expect it.
